// Services is a collection of services that are used by the auth server.
// Avoid using this type as a dependency and instead depend on the actual
// methods/services you need. It should really only be necessary to directly
// reference this type on auth.Server itself and on code that manages
// the lifecycle of the auth server.

content.text: Scoping down: `integrations/` plugins and cloud SDK clients hold gRPC API clients, not the storage layer. Focusing on `lib/` + `tool/`.

input.command:
cd /tmp/audit && grep -E "^(lib|tool)/" hits2.txt | grep -vE "gcp\.|awsconfig\.|msgraph|AzureToken|SQLAdmin|RolesAnywhere|OIDCIntegrationClient|iface (teleport|common)\.|CacheAWSOIDC" | grep -vE "authclient\.ClientI" | wc -l
grep -E "^(lib|tool)/" hits2.txt | grep -vE "^lib/(services|cache|backend)/" | grep -vE "gcp\.|awsconfig\.|msgraph|AzureToken|SQLAdmin|RolesAnywhere|OIDCIntegrationClient|iface (teleport|common)\.|CacheAWSOIDC|authclient\.ClientI" | cut -f1 | cut -d/ -f1-3 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

content.text:
I have a complete, type-checked picture. Here's the audit.

---

# Audit: production holders of a cluster-state storage handle

## Method (so you can reproduce / re-run after the rework)

Two independent sweeps over all 4,266 non-test `.go` files, then unioned:

1. **AST sweep** — every struct field whose declared type is `lib/services.*`, `lib/cache.Cache`, `auth.Services`, `authclient.*AccessPoint`, or a *locally declared* interface transitively carrying a storage-shaped method (`NewWatcher`, `Get*/List*` taking `context.Context`).
2. **Type-checked sweep** (`go/packages` + `types.Implements`) — computed the full set of interfaces in the repo that `*cache.Cache`, `*auth.Services` or `*auth.Server` satisfy (**379 interfaces**), then found every struct field typed with one. This is what catches the holders that never import the storage package.

Sweep 2 found **206 holders sweep 1 missed** (notably all of `lib/web`, `lib/auth/webauthn`, the `spiffeFederation*`/`workloadIdentity*` readers, `lib/srv/discovery` internals). Sweep 1 found **139** sweep 2 missed (concrete `*services.LockWatcher`-style fields, which aren't interfaces).

Excluded deliberately: audit-log/event-emission handles (`events.Emitter`, `AuditLogger`, `MultipartHandler`, session-recording blob handlers), value types that are not handles (`services.AccessChecker`, `services.CommandLabels`, `services.ResourceMatcher`), and `integrations/*` plugins + `examples/` (they hold the gRPC `teleport.Client`, not the storage layer). Raw `backend.Backend` (KV layer *under* the resource layer) is listed separately in §L.

---

## A. The layer itself — definitional aggregates

These are the contracts you're reworking, listed first because every entry below reaches storage through one of them.

| Holder | file:line | Reaches storage via |
|---|---|---|
| `auth.Services` — 70 embedded service interfaces | `lib/auth/services.go:34` (fields `:35`–`:104`) | **is** the backend aggregate; each field is one `lib/services/local` service |
| `cache.Config` — ~55 upstream reader fields | `lib/cache/cache.go:688` (fields `:700`–`:852`) | holds one `services.X` reader per collection the cache replicates |
| `accesspoint.Config` — 53 fields | `lib/auth/accesspoint/accesspoint.go:42` (fields `:72`–`:124`, plus `Events` at `:89`) | the struct that builds every component cache; one `services.X` per replicated resource |
| `auth.InitConfig` — ~60 service fields | `lib/auth/init.go:107` (fields `:112`–`:484`) | bootstrap-time handles to every backend service |

## The two things I'd flag for the rework

1. **§D is where a contract change bites hardest.** ~30 auth gRPC service packages each declare a private `Backend`/`Cache`/`Reader` interface and retain it on *two* structs. Because they're structurally typed and never import `lib/cache` or `lib/services/local`, a method-signature change compiles cleanly at the interface site and fails only at the wiring call in `lib/auth/auth.go` / `lib/service/service.go` — or, if the method is dropped, fails nowhere and panics at runtime on a nil interface.

2. **"How long a handle stays valid" has real multi-handle holders.** `leafCluster` (`lib/reversetunnel/leaf_cluster.go:86`,`:89`) holds a local *and* a remote cache with independent lifetimes; the eleven `*Wrapper` structs in §B each hold a cached and an uncached handle; `sessionCache.accessPoint` (`lib/web/sessions.go:707`) outlives every individual web session it serves. Those are the four places where a lifetime/close-semantics change is most likely to leak or use-after-close.

**Verification caveat:** the enterprise submodule `e/` is empty in this checkout, so nothing under it is covered. Scan scripts are at `/tmp/audit/main.go` (AST) and `/tmp/audit2/main.go` (type-checked); re-running the second one after the rework and diffing the 379-interface set is the cheapest regression check.

# Audit: production holders of a cluster-state storage handle

## Method (so you can reproduce / re-run after the rework)

Two independent sweeps over all 4,266 non-test `.go` files, then unioned:

1. **AST sweep** — every struct field whose declared type is `lib/services.*`, `lib/cache.Cache`, `auth.Services`, `authclient.*AccessPoint`, or a *locally declared* interface transitively carrying a storage-shaped method (`NewWatcher`, `Get*/List*` taking `context.Context`).
2. **Type-checked sweep** (`go/packages` + `types.Implements`) — computed the full set of interfaces in the repo that `*cache.Cache`, `*auth.Services` or `*auth.Server` satisfy (**379 interfaces**), then found every struct field typed with one. This is what catches the holders that never import the storage package.

Sweep 2 found **206 holders sweep 1 missed** (notably all of `lib/web`, `lib/auth/webauthn`, the `spiffeFederation*`/`workloadIdentity*` readers, `lib/srv/discovery` internals). Sweep 1 found **139** sweep 2 missed (concrete `*services.LockWatcher`-style fields, which aren't interfaces).

Excluded deliberately: audit-log/event-emission handles (`events.Emitter`, `AuditLogger`, `MultipartHandler`, session-recording blob handlers), value types that are not handles (`services.AccessChecker`, `services.CommandLabels`, `services.ResourceMatcher`), and `integrations/*` plugins + `examples/` (they hold the gRPC `teleport.Client`, not the storage layer). Raw `backend.Backend` (KV layer *under* the resource layer) is listed separately in §L.

## The two things I'd flag for the rework

1. **§D is where a contract change bites hardest.** ~30 auth gRPC service packages each declare a private `Backend`/`Cache`/`Reader` interface and retain it on *two* structs. Because they're structurally typed and never import `lib/cache` or `lib/services/local`, a method-signature change compiles cleanly at the interface site and fails only at the wiring call in `lib/auth/auth.go` / `lib/service/service.go` — or, if the method is dropped, fails nowhere and panics at runtime on a nil interface.

2. **"How long a handle stays valid" has real multi-handle holders.** `leafCluster` (`lib/reversetunnel/leaf_cluster.go:86`,`:89`) holds a local *and* a remote cache with independent lifetimes; the eleven `*Wrapper` structs in §B each hold a cached and an uncached handle; `sessionCache.accessPoint` (`lib/web/sessions.go:707`) outlives every individual web session it serves. Those are the four places where a lifetime/close-semantics change is most likely to leak or use-after-close.

**Verification caveat:** the enterprise submodule `e/` is empty in this checkout, so nothing under it is covered. Scan scripts are at `/tmp/audit/main.go` (AST) and `/tmp/audit2/main.go` (type-checked); re-running the second one after the rework and diffing the 379-interface set is the cheapest regression check.
